definitions.definition: An inequality involving a linear expression in one or more variables. It is of the form ax + b > c, ax + b < c, ax + b ≥ c, or ax + b ≤ c, where a, b, and c are constants and x is the variable.
definitions.example: For example, 2x + 3 > 7 is a linear inequality in one variable x.

definitions.definition: An expression of the form ax + b, where a and b are constants and x is the variable.
definitions.example: For example, 2x + 3 is a linear expression in one variable x.

definitions.definition: A constant that is multiplied by a variable in a term. In a linear expression, the coefficient of the variable is the number in front of the variable.
definitions.example: In the linear expression 2x + 3, the coefficient of x is 2.

definitions.definition: A letter or symbol that represents a value that can change in an expression or equation.
definitions.example: In the linear expression 2x + 3, the variable is x.

definitions.definition: A value that makes an inequality true.
definitions.example: For example, if the inequality is 2x + 3 > 7, then x = 2 is a solution.

definitions.definition: The set of all solutions to an inequality.
definitions.example: For example, if the inequality is 2x + 3 > 7, then the solution set is all real numbers x such that x > 2.

definitions.definition: A way to write the solution set of an inequality using intervals on the number line.
definitions.example: For example, the solution set of the inequality x > 2 can be written as (2, ∞) in interval notation.

definitions.definition: A line that represents all real numbers, with points on the line representing each real number.
definitions.example: For example, the number line is a line with points labeled 0, 1, 2, 3, ... and so on.

definitions.definition: A set of real numbers that includes all numbers between two given numbers, including the endpoints.
definitions.example: For example, the interval [1, 5] includes all numbers from 1 to 5, including 1 and 5.

definitions.definition: A number that is included in an interval.
definitions.example: For example, in the interval [1, 5], the endpoints are 1 and 5.

definitions.definition: An interval that does not include its endpoints.
definitions.example: For example, the open interval (1, 5) does not include 1 and 5.

definitions.definition: An interval that includes its endpoints.
definitions.example: For example, the closed interval [1, 5] includes 1 and 5.

definitions.definition: A visual representation of the solution set of an inequality.
definitions.example: For example, the graph of the inequality x > 2 is a line that is shaded to the right of 2.

definitions.definition: An inequality that involves two inequalities, such as a ≤ x ≤ b or a ≥ x ≥ b.
definitions.example: For example, the double inequality 2 ≤ x ≤ 5 is an inequality that involves two inequalities.

definitions.definition: An inequality that involves a linear expression in one variable.
definitions.example: For example, the inequality 2x + 3 > 7 is a linear inequality in one variable x.

definitions.definition: An inequality that involves a linear expression in two variables.
definitions.example: For example, the inequality 2x + 3y > 7 is a linear inequality in two variables x and y.

definitions.definition: A set of linear inequalities in one or more variables.
definitions.example: For example, the system of linear inequalities {2x + 3 > 7, x + 2 ≥ 3} is a system of linear inequalities.

definitions.definition: A method for optimizing a linear function subject to a set of linear inequalities.
definitions.example: For example, linear programming can be used to optimize the profit of a company subject to a set of linear inequalities representing the available resources.
